linting.notes: golangci-lint uses extensive linter configuration with 50+ enabled linters. Ruff is used for Python linting with select rules (B, E, F, W). The project uses 'make precommit' which runs all linting, formatting, and code generation.

go.run_command_direct: KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS=$(bin/setup-envtest use 1.34 -p path) go test --timeout 30m $(go list ./pkg/...) ./cmd/... -coverprofile coverage.out
go.notes: Tests use envtest to create a minimal Kubernetes API server. Some tests require manifest generation which needs kubectl.

python.run_command: pytest in each python package directory
python.notes: Multiple Python packages: kserve (SDK), storage, sklearnserver, xgbserver, lgbserver, pmmlserver, paddleserver, huggingfaceserver. Each has its own venv and test suite.

gating_checks.name: Precommit Check
gating_checks.description: Runs make precommit and verifies no changes, includes linting, formatting, code generation

gating_checks.name: Go Test
gating_checks.description: Runs Go unit tests with coverage reporting

gating_checks.name: Python package
gating_checks.description: Runs pytest for kserve, storage, and all server packages on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12

advisory_checks.name: E2E Test
advisory_checks.description: Full end-to-end tests with Kubernetes cluster

ci.notes: CI uses merge strategy for PRs. The 'precommit-check' workflow is the primary gating check that validates all code is properly formatted, linted, and generated code is up to date.

go.install_command: go mod download && cd qpext && go mod download

container_build.python_servers: cd python && docker buildx build -f sklearn.Dockerfile -t {KO_DOCKER_REPO}/{SKLEARN_IMG} .

build.notes: Multi-language build. Go uses standard go toolchain. Python uses uv for dependency management. Project has extensive container build targets for controllers and prediction servers.

conventions.notes: Go code follows controller-runtime patterns. Python follows standard pytest conventions. Both use strict linting configurations.

lint_commands.notes: Linter found violations in cmd/llmisvc/main.go (typecheck and unused import). This is a legitimate lint failure, not a broken command.

test_commands.notes: Tests pass successfully. Sample tests validated: InferenceGraph validation, LLMInferenceService conversion.
Requires kubectl for manifest generation (config/llmisvc/gateway-inference-extension.yaml). Tests run successfully when kubectl is available.

go.notes: GOTOOLCHAIN is auto-selected from go.mod. Tests use envtest for Kubernetes API mocking.

lint_commands.command: pip install ruff && ruff check --config ruff.toml python/kserve/kserve
lint_commands.notes: All checks passed! Python code is clean.

test_commands.notes: 9 tests passed in 0.07s. Tests validated: service account creation, credential management.

python.notes: Python tests for ray/llm features require additional dependencies (ray, vllm). Some tests may require storage backend access.

Python linting passed with no violations

validation.summary: Go: deps OK, lint OK (found violations), tests OK. Python: deps OK, lint OK (clean), tests OK (9 passed).

gaps: Full 'make test' requires kubectl for manifest generation (gateway-inference-extension CRDs)
Full 'make check' requires all tools to be installed (controller-gen, yq, helm-docs, etc.)
Python ray/llm tests require vLLM and ray[serve] which have large dependencies
E2E tests require a full Kubernetes cluster (minikube or kind)
Some controller tests may require actual Kubernetes resources beyond envtest mocking
Python huggingfaceserver tests require GPU or special CPU features for vLLM
Full CI workflow validates generated code is committed (client code, CRDs, manifests)
